revision.comment: 新しいページ：ニゴレ古墳（京都府京丹後市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = ニゴレ古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京丹後市]]弥栄町鳥取（字ニゴレ） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 47.75 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 3.75 |ISO = JP-26 |形状 = 不整形墳 |規模 = 墳丘長20m
高さ3-4m |埋葬施設 = [[舟形木棺]]直葬 |出土品 = 鉄剣・鉄鏃・甲冑・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto |アイコン = |ラベル = ニゴレ古墳 |ラベル位置 = right }} '''ニゴレ古墳'''（ニゴレこふん）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]弥栄町鳥取にある[[古墳]]。形状は不整形墳。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 京都府北部、[[竹野川]]中流域西岸、竹野川支流の鳥取川流域の低丘陵端（標高約40メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に旧弥栄町教育委員会・[[京都大学]]による発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。 墳形は不整形で、墳丘長20メートル・高さ3-4メートルを測る{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。墳丘外表で段築・[[葺石]]は認められないが、北側の丘陵切り離し溝部では東西方向の[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）が検出されている{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。また墳頂部は南北約10メートル・東西約6メートルの平坦面を呈し、付近から[[形象埴輪]]（家形6・甲冑形1・船形1・椅子形1）が検出されている{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。埋葬施設は墳頂部中央における[[舟形木棺]]の直葬である{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。墓壙は2段で、主軸を東西方向として、中央に木棺を据える{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。遺骸は東枕で、頭位から鉄製甲冑、左側から刀剣、足位から鉄鏃束がそれぞれ副葬品として検出されている{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。丹後半島では甲冑が出土した数少ない古墳の1つであるとともに、一帯は竹野川流域（弥栄町）から[[離湖]]（網野町）へ抜ける交通路上であり、海上交通を示唆する準構造船を模した船形埴輪の出土の点でも注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}{{Sfn|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第2集|2019}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|甲冑}}{{small|[[京都大学総合博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|船形埴輪（複製）}}{{small|京丹後市立丹後古代の里資料館展示。}}]] 埋葬施設から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。 * 武器 ** 鉄剣 2 ** 鉄鏃 11 * 武具 ** 小札革綴衝角付冑 ** 板錣 ** 頸甲 ** 肩甲 ** 三角板革綴短甲 ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|衝角付冑・頸甲 ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|肩甲・短甲 == 関連施設 == * [[京都大学総合博物館]]（京都市左京区吉田本町） - ニゴレ古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 * 京丹後市立丹後古代の里資料館（京丹後市丹後町宮） - ニゴレ古墳出土の船形埴輪複製を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京丹後市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=鳥取村|title=京都府の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]26|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490263|ref={{Harvid|鳥取村（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=佐藤晃一|year=1989|chapter=ニゴレ古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|ニゴレ古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=ニゴレ古墳|title=京丹後市の考古資料|volume=京丹後市史資料編|publisher=京丹後市|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2019|chapter=ニゴレ古墳|title=京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック|volume=第2集|publisher=京都府教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第2集|2019}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=天理大学博物館学研究室|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=ニゴレ古墳|volume=京都府弥栄町文化財調査報告第5集|publisher=弥栄町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=弥栄ニゴレ古墳とその周辺|volume=特別陳列図録24|publisher=京都府立丹後郷土資料|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=小笠原好彦|title=家形埴輪の文様 -京都府ニゴレ古墳の場合-|date=1989-04|journal=郵政考古紀要|publisher=郵政考古学会|volume=|number=14|pages=39-48|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} {{デフォルトソート:にこれこふん}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]]

高さ3-4m |埋葬施設 = [[舟形木棺]]直葬 |出土品 = 鉄剣・鉄鏃・甲冑・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto |アイコン = |ラベル = ニゴレ古墳 |ラベル位置 = right }} '''ニゴレ古墳'''（ニゴレこふん）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]弥栄町鳥取にある[[古墳]]。形状は不整形墳。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 京都府北部、[[竹野川]]中流域西岸、竹野川支流の鳥取川流域の低丘陵端（標高約40メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に旧弥栄町教育委員会・[[京都大学]]による発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。 墳形は不整形で、墳丘長20メートル・高さ3-4メートルを測る{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。墳丘外表で段築・[[葺石]]は認められないが、北側の丘陵切り離し溝部では東西方向の[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）が検出されている{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。また墳頂部は南北約10メートル・東西約6メートルの平坦面を呈し、付近から[[形象埴輪]]（家形6・甲冑形1・船形1・椅子形1）が検出されている{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。埋葬施設は墳頂部中央における[[舟形木棺]]の直葬である{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。墓壙は2段で、主軸を東西方向として、中央に木棺を据える{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。遺骸は東枕で、頭位から完形の鉄製甲冑、左側から刀剣、足位から鉄鏃束がそれぞれ副葬品として検出されている{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。丹後半島では甲冑が出土した数少ない古墳の1つであるとともに、一帯は竹野川流域（弥栄町）から[[離湖]]（網野町）へ抜ける交通路上であり、海上交通を示唆する準構造船を模した船形埴輪の出土の点でも注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}{{Sfn|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第2集|2019}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|甲冑}}{{small|[[京都大学総合博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|船形埴輪（複製）}}{{small|京丹後市立丹後古代の里資料館展示。}}]] 埋葬施設から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}。 * 武器 ** 鉄剣 2 ** 鉄鏃 11 * 武具 ** 小札革綴衝角付冑 ** 板錣 ** 頸甲 ** 肩甲 ** 三角板革綴短甲 ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|衝角付冑・板錣 ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|頸甲・肩甲・短甲 == 関連施設 == * [[京都大学総合博物館]]（京都市左京区吉田本町） - ニゴレ古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 * 京丹後市立丹後古代の里資料館（京丹後市丹後町宮） - ニゴレ古墳出土の船形埴輪複製を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（京丹後市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=鳥取村|title=京都府の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]26|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490263|ref={{Harvid|鳥取村（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=佐藤晃一|year=1989|chapter=ニゴレ古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|ニゴレ古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=ニゴレ古墳|title=京丹後市の考古資料|volume=京丹後市史資料編|publisher=京丹後市|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|京丹後市の考古資料|2010}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2019|chapter=ニゴレ古墳|title=京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック|volume=第2集|publisher=京都府教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第2集|2019}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=天理大学博物館学研究室|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=ニゴレ古墳|volume=京都府弥栄町文化財調査報告第5集|publisher=弥栄町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=弥栄ニゴレ古墳とその周辺|volume=特別陳列図録24|publisher=京都府立丹後郷土資料|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=小笠原好彦|title=家形埴輪の文様 -京都府ニゴレ古墳の場合-|date=1989-04|journal=郵政考古紀要|publisher=郵政考古学会|volume=|number=14|pages=39-48|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} {{デフォルトソート:にこれこふん}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto#Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient **kofun burial mound** located in **Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. ## **Overview** Nigore Kofun is situated in **northern Kyoto Prefecture**, on the **western bank of the Takenogawa River**, near the **Tottorigawa tributary** at an elevation of approximately **40 meters**. It was excavated in **1958** by the former **Yasaka Town Board of Education** and **Kyoto University**.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an **irregular shape**, measuring approximately **20 meters in length** and **3-4 meters in height**. There is **no evidence of tiered construction** or **fukiishi** (cover stones), but an array of **cylindrical haniwa**, including **morning-glory-shaped haniwa**, was discovered on the **north side of the burial mound**. The **summit** is relatively flat, measuring **10 meters north-south** and **6 meters east-west**, where **house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa** were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a **direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial** with an **east-west orientation**. The **deceased was laid with their head facing east**, and several burial artifacts were found: **a complete iron cuirass near the head**, **swords along the left side**, and **bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet**. The mound dates to the **mid-5th century**, during the **Middle Kofun period**. It is one of the **few kofun in the Tango Peninsula** where **armor** has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an **important transportation route** linking the **Takenogawa River Basin** to **Lake Riko** and the **coastal region** suggests its association with **maritime activities**, particularly given the presence of a **boat-shaped haniwa**.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- ## **Artifacts** The burial chamber contained **iron weapons and armor**, including: * **Weapons:** * Two **iron swords** * Eleven **iron arrowheads** * **Armor:** * **Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)** * **Iron plating for neck guard** * **Shoulder guards** * **Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)** ### **Gallery** File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- ## **Related Facilities** Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * **[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)** – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * **Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)** – Displays a **replica of the boat-shaped haniwa**. --- ## **References** {{Reflist}} --- ## **Further Reading** * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- ## **See Also** * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto) ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Nigore Kofun|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:ニゴレ古墳]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto#Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient **kofun burial mound** located in **Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. ## **Overview** Nigore Kofun is situated in **northern Kyoto Prefecture**, on the **western bank of the Takenogawa River**, near the **Tottorigawa tributary** at an elevation of approximately **40 meters**. It was excavated in **1958** by the former **Yasaka Town Board of Education** and **Kyoto University**.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an **irregular shape**, measuring approximately **20 meters in length** and **3-4 meters in height**. There is **no evidence of tiered construction** or **fukiishi** (cover stones), but an array of **cylindrical haniwa**, including **morning-glory-shaped haniwa**, was discovered on the **north side of the burial mound**. The **summit** is relatively flat, measuring **10 meters north-south** and **6 meters east-west**, where **house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa** were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a **direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial** with an **east-west orientation**. The **deceased was laid with their head facing east**, and several burial artifacts were found: **a complete iron cuirass near the head**, **swords along the left side**, and **bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet**. The mound dates to the **mid-5th century**, during the **Middle Kofun period**. It is one of the **few kofun in the Tango Peninsula** where **armor** has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an **important transportation route** linking the **Takenogawa River Basin** to **Lake Riko** and the **coastal region** suggests its association with **maritime activities**, particularly given the presence of a **boat-shaped haniwa**.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- ## **Artifacts** The burial chamber contained **iron weapons and armor**, including: * **Weapons:** * Two **iron swords** * Eleven **iron arrowheads** * **Armor:** * **Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)** * **Iron plating for neck guard** * **Shoulder guards** * **Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)** ### **Gallery** File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- ## **Related Facilities** Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * **[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)** – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * **Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)** – Displays a **replica of the boat-shaped haniwa**. --- ## **References** {{Reflist}} --- ## **Further Reading** * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. ## **Overview** Nigore Kofun is situated in **northern Kyoto Prefecture**, on the **western bank of the Takenogawa River**, near the **Tottorigawa tributary** at an elevation of approximately **40 meters**. It was excavated in **1958** by the former **Yasaka Town Board of Education** and **Kyoto University**.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an **irregular shape**, measuring approximately **20 meters in length** and **3-4 meters in height**. There is **no evidence of tiered construction** or **fukiishi** (cover stones), but an array of **cylindrical haniwa**, including **morning-glory-shaped haniwa**, was discovered on the **north side of the burial mound**. The **summit** is relatively flat, measuring **10 meters north-south** and **6 meters east-west**, where **house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa** were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a **direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial** with an **east-west orientation**. The **deceased was laid with their head facing east**, and several burial artifacts were found: **a complete iron cuirass near the head**, **swords along the left side**, and **bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet**. The mound dates to the **mid-5th century**, during the **Middle Kofun period**. It is one of the **few kofun in the Tango Peninsula** where **armor** has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an **important transportation route** linking the **Takenogawa River Basin** to **Lake Riko** and the **coastal region** suggests its association with **maritime activities**, particularly given the presence of a **boat-shaped haniwa**.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- ## **Artifacts** The burial chamber contained **iron weapons and armor**, including: * **Weapons:** * Two **iron swords** * Eleven **iron arrowheads** * **Armor:** * **Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)** * **Iron plating for neck guard** * **Shoulder guards** * **Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)** ### **Gallery** File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun

The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. ## **Overview** Nigore Kofun is situated in **northern Kyoto Prefecture**, on the **western bank of the Takenogawa River**, near the **Tottorigawa tributary** at an elevation of approximately **40 meters**. It was excavated in **1958** by the former **Yasaka Town Board of Education** and **Kyoto University**.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an **irregular shape**, measuring approximately **20 meters in length** and **3-4 meters in height**. There is **no evidence of tiered construction** or **fukiishi** (cover stones), but an array of **cylindrical haniwa**, including **morning-glory-shaped haniwa**, was discovered on the **north side of the burial mound**. The **summit** is relatively flat, measuring **10 meters north-south** and **6 meters east-west**, where **house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa** were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a **direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial** with an **east-west orientation**. The **deceased was laid with their head facing east**, and several burial artifacts were found: **a complete iron cuirass near the head**, **swords along the left side**, and **bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet**. The mound dates to the **mid-5th century**, during the **Middle Kofun period**. It is one of the **few kofun in the Tango Peninsula** where **armor** has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an **important transportation route** linking the **Takenogawa River Basin** to **Lake Riko** and the **coastal region** suggests its association with **maritime activities**, particularly given the presence of a **boat-shaped haniwa**.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- ## **Artifacts** The burial chamber contained **iron weapons and armor**, including: * **Weapons:** * Two **iron swords** * Eleven **iron arrowheads** * **Armor:** * **Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)** * **Iron plating for neck guard** * **Shoulder guards** * **Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)** ### **Gallery** File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- ## **Related Facilities** Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * **[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)** – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * **Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)** – Displays a **replica of the boat-shaped haniwa**. --- ## **References** {{Reflist}} --- ## **Further Reading** * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- ## **See Also** * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto) ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Nigore Kofun|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Nigore Kofun-qq]] [[ja:ニゴレ古墳]]

The tomb’s location on an **important transportation route** linking the **Takenogawa River Basin** to **Lake Riko** and the **coastal region** suggests its association with **maritime activities**, particularly given the presence of a **boat-shaped haniwa**.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- ## **Artifacts** The burial chamber contained **iron weapons and armor**, including: * **Weapons:** * Two **iron swords** * Eleven **iron arrowheads** * **Armor:** * **Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)** * **Iron plating for neck guard** * **Shoulder guards** * **Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)** ### **Gallery** File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto#Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient **kofun burial mound** located in **Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. ## **Overview** Nigore Kofun is situated in **northern Kyoto Prefecture**, on the **western bank of the Takenogawa River**, near the **Tottorigawa tributary** at an elevation of approximately **40 meters**. It was excavated in **1958** by the former **Yasaka Town Board of Education** and **Kyoto University**.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an **irregular shape**, measuring approximately **20 meters in length** and **3-4 meters in height**. There is **no evidence of tiered construction** or **fukiishi** (cover stones), but an array of **cylindrical haniwa**, including **morning-glory-shaped haniwa**, was discovered on the **north side of the burial mound**. The **summit** is relatively flat, measuring **10 meters north-south** and **6 meters east-west**, where **house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa** were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a **direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial** with an **east-west orientation**. The **deceased was laid with their head facing east**, and several burial artifacts were found: **a complete iron cuirass near the head**, **swords along the left side**, and **bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet**. The mound dates to the **mid-5th century**, during the **Middle Kofun period**. It is one of the **few kofun in the Tango Peninsula** where **armor** has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an **important transportation route** linking the **Takenogawa River Basin** to **Lake Riko** and the **coastal region** suggests its association with **maritime activities**, particularly given the presence of a **boat-shaped haniwa**.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- ## **Artifacts** The burial chamber contained **iron weapons and armor**, including: * **Weapons:** * Two **iron swords** * Eleven **iron arrowheads** * **Armor:** * **Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)** * **Iron plating for neck guard** * **Shoulder guards** * **Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)** === **Gallery** File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- ## **Related Facilities** Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * **[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)** – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * **Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)** – Displays a **replica of the boat-shaped haniwa**. --- ## **References** {{Reflist}} --- ## **Further Reading** * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- ## **See Also** * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto) ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Nigore Kofun|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Nigore Kofun-qq]] [[ja:ニゴレ古墳]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun burial mound''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto) ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Nigore Kofun|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Nigore Kofun-qq]] [[ja:ニゴレ古墳]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun burial mound''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto) [[File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Nigore Kofun|en}}]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun burial mound''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun burial mound''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun burial mound''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun burial mound''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)

There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun burial mound''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped burial mound | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun burial mound''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the burial mound'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Burial Mounds Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' * Two '''iron swords''' * Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' * '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' * '''Iron plating for neck guard''' * '''Shoulder guards''' * '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:Nigore Kofun Armor.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:Nigore Kofun Boat-shaped Haniwa Replica.JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:Nigore Kofun Kabuto.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:Nigore Kofun Tanki.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned Kabuto

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned Kabuto

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = Location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | artifacts = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in shape and has not been designated as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the former '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular shape''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial artifacts were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''Artifacts''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == Artifacts from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original artifacts from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historical Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nigore Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyotango]] [[Category:Japanese archaeological sites]] Horned Kabuto
