Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | burial goods = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | burial goods = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] Horned Kabuto

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | burial goods = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] Horned Kabuto

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[Kyōtango]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | burial goods = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * [[Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] Horned Kabuto

There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] Horned Kabuto

The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | burial goods = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. [[sn:commons:Category:Nigore Kofun]][[ja:ニゴレ古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q127642501]] Wikidata name: Nigore Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | burial goods = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] Horned Kabuto

The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)

The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)

The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nigore Kofun]][[ja:ニゴレ古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nigore Kofun]][[ja:ニゴレ古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nigore Kofun]][[ja:ニゴレ古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q127642501]] Wikidata name: Nigore Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | burial goods = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''[[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]] * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] Horned Kabuto

The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|WD=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum}}) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)

[[co:commons:Category:Nigore Kofun]][[ja:ニゴレ古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nigore Kofun]][[ja:ニゴレ古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nigore Kofun]][[ja:ニゴレ古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nigore Kofun | native_name = ニゴレ古墳 | image = | map_type = Japan Kyoto=Japan | map_caption = location of Nigore Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|47.75|N|135|4|3.75|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} | location = Tottori, Yasaka-chō, {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan | type = Irregularly shaped Kofun | length = 20m | height = 3-4m | built = Mid-5th century | period = [[Kofun period]] | burial = Direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial | discovered = 1958 | excavation = [[Kyoto University]] | burial goods = Iron sword, iron arrowheads, armor, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} | public_access = No }} '''Nigore Kofun''' (ニゴレ古墳) is an ancient '''kofun Kofun''' located in '''Yasaka-chō, Kyōtango, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|WD=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum}}) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''{{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|WD=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum}} (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|WD=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum}} * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] Horned Kabuto
{{small|Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture. Exhibited at the Kyoto Unive ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|ニゴレ古墳|version=101839186|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:ニゴレ古墳]] {{Commons category|Nigore Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q127642501}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

The tumulus is irregular in type and has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == '''Overview''' == Nigore Kofun is situated in '''northern Kyoto Prefecture''', on the '''western bank of the Takenogawa River''', near the '''Tottorigawa tributary''' at an elevation of approximately '''40 meters'''. It was excavated in '''1958''' by the typeer '''Yasaka Town Board of Education''' and '''Kyoto University'''.Kyotango Archaeological Records, 2010 The tumulus has an '''irregular type''', measuring approximately '''20 meters in length''' and '''3-4 meters in height'''. There is '''no evidence of tiered construction''' or '''fukiishi''' (cover stones), but an array of '''cylindrical haniwa''', including '''morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', was discovered on the '''north side of the Kofun'''. The '''summit''' is relatively flat, measuring '''10 meters north-south''' and '''6 meters east-west''', where '''house-shaped, armor-shaped, boat-shaped, and chair-shaped haniwa''' were unearthed. The burial chamber contained a '''direct wooden boat-shaped coffin burial''' with an '''east-west orientation'''. The '''deceased was laid with their head facing east''', and several burial burial goods were found: '''a complete iron cuirass near the head''', '''swords along the left side''', and '''bundles of iron arrowheads near the feet'''. The mound dates to the '''mid-5th century''', during the '''Middle Kofun period'''. It is one of the '''few kofun in the Tango Peninsula''' where '''armor''' has been unearthed. The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun
(Displayed at {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|WD=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}}) File:ニゴレ古墳 船形埴輪 (複製).JPG|Boat-shaped haniwa (replica)
(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''{{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|WD=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}} (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2. Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, 2019. * Kyotango Archaeological Records. Kyotango City Board of Education, 2010. * "Nigore Kofun." *Japanese Ancient Kofuns Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing, 1989. --- == '''See Also''' == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|WD=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}} * [[Maritime archaeology of Japan]] Horned Kabuto

The tomb’s location on an '''important transportation route''' linking the '''Takenogawa River Basin''' to '''Lake Riko''' and the '''coastal region''' suggests its association with '''maritime activities''', particularly given the presence of a '''boat-shaped haniwa'''.Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, 2019 --- == '''burial goods''' == The burial chamber contained '''iron weapons and armor''', including: * '''Weapons:''' ** Two '''iron swords''' ** Eleven '''iron arrowheads''' * '''Armor:''' ** '''Small-laced leather lamellar helmet (Shokaku-fukutochi kabuto)''' ** '''Iron plating for neck guard''' ** '''Shoulder guards''' ** '''Triangular-plated leather lamellar cuirass (Tanki armor)''' === '''Gallery''' === File:ニゴレ古墳 甲冑.JPG|Iron armor from Nigore Kofun

(Displayed at Kyotango City Museum) File:ニゴレ古墳 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet and iron plating File:ニゴレ古墳 短甲.JPG|Neck guard, shoulder armor, and cuirass --- == '''Related Facilities''' == burial goods from Nigore Kofun are housed and displayed at: * '''{{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|WD=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}} (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward)''' – Houses original burial goods from Nigore Kofun. * '''Kyotango City Museum of Ancient History (Kyotango, Tango Town, Miya)''' – Displays a '''replica of the boat-shaped haniwa'''. --- == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} --- == '''Further Reading''' == * Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites Handbook, Vol. 2.
